patch.description:
Certain updates on Entity trigger KYC re-screening, requiring an updated kyc_details.result and kyc_details.screened_at.

For individual entities, KYC re-screening is triggered by changes to: first name, last name, date of birth, tax ID (for US entities), existing identification document (for non-US entities), and switching between US vs. non-US entities.

For business and sole proprietorship entities, KYC re-screening is triggered by changes to: business name, business type, tax ID (US entities), and existing identification document (non-US entities).

Note: Business and sole proprietors cannot switch between US and non-US entities. A new entity must be created in such case.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Idempotency key

subledger_balance_id.description: Unique ID of the credit subledger balance object that a funding object is being created for. The referenced Subledger Balance must be in an `active` status. If the Subledger Balance has an `expected_maturity_date`, it must not be in the past.

principal.description: The total amount of funding that is part of the loan product's principal. All amounts are in minor units. If the referenced Subledger Balance has a `details.structure` of `non_revolving`, the sum of all `principal.*` fields on this request plus the sum of `principal.*` fields on any prior Funding in a `posted` or `processing` status associated with that Subledger Balance must not exceed the Subledger Balance's `max_funding_amount`.

external_disbursement_amount.description: The portion of the principal amount that will be made available for external disbursement at the time of funding (in minor units). Either this field or `withheld_amount` must be greater than 0.

withheld_amount.description: The portion of the principal amount that will be withheld and not disbursed externally at the time of funding (in minor units). Either this field or `external_disbursement_amount` must be greater than 0.

merchant_origination_fee_amount.description: The amount of origination fees charged to the merchant that is included in the principal amount (in minor units). Must be less than `external_disbursement_amount` + `withheld_amount`.

other_fees.description: Fees relating to the funding request that Lead needs awareness of for bookkeeping purposes only.

borrower_origination_fee_amount.description: Non-capitalized origination fee charged to the borrower at the point of funding (in minor units).

metadata.description: Additional metadata associated with the funding.

How fast you want the counterparty to receive the ACH.
* `same_business_day`: if the ACH request is submitted before the cutoff window with the same business day option, funds will settle on the same day. 
* `next_business_day`: standard ACH processing, for funds to settle on the next business day.

PaymentMethod.description: The payment method chosen for disbursement. Only ACH is supported today.

counterparty.description: The details of the counterparty you are sending money to.

name.description: The name of the counterparty you are transacting with.

account_number.description: The account number for the bank account.

routing_number.description: The routing number for the bank account. This should be the ACH routing number, not the wire routing number.

DisbursementStatus.description: The current status of this Disbursement object.

failure_code.description: The failure code for failed disbursement. This applies in the case of rejected disbursements either by the Fed, ODFI or RDFI.

failure_details.description:
Details on failure reason and recommended next steps for failed disbursements.
* `account_closed`: This account is closed. Please contact the account owner and request valid bank account details.
* `no_account`: This account cannot be located. Please contact the account owner and request valid bank account details.
* `bad_account_number`: This account contains invalid account number structure. Please contact the account owner and request valid bank account details.
* `account_frozen`: This account is frozen and funds cannot be disbursed.
* `contact_lead`: We were unable to complete the disbursement. Please contact Lead for more information.

effective_date.description: The date the ACH transaction is expected to settle with the financial institution. Lead will set this date based on the delivery_type field.

trace_number.description: The unique number assigned to every ACH entry by an ODFI which identifies that entry within a specific ACH file. This is generated by Lead when we submit the ACH.

counterparty.description: The details of the counterparty you are sending money to.

name.description: The name of the counterparty you are transacting with.

account_number.description: The account number for the bank account.

routing_number.description: The routing number for the bank account. This should be the ACH routing number, not the wire routing number.

created_at.description: The ISO-8601 timestamp at which the Disbursement object was created.

updated_at.description: The ISO-8601 timestamp at which the Disbursement object was last updated.

amount.description: Amount disbursed to the counterparty in cents. This amount will align with disbursement_amount from the Loan Origination Request

SenderName.description: The name of the sender. Maximum number of characters is 16.

SenderCompanyID.description: The company id of the sender. Maximum number of characters is 10.

SenderRoutingNumber.description: The 9 digit routing number of the sender.

InternalTransferStatusV1.description: The current status of the InternalTransfer object.

reason.description: The rejection code that indicates the type of rejection

id.description: The ID of the Internal Transfer object.

created_at.description: The RFC3339 timestamp at which the InternalTransfer object was created.

updated_at.description: The RFC3339 timestamp at which the InternalTransfer object was last updated.

InternalTransferAmount.description: The amount of the internal transfer transaction in cents.

WireDirection.description:
Who is initiating the transaction.

outgoing: You are sending a wire transaction to a counterparty.
incoming: You are receiving an wire transaction from a counterparty.

WireStatus.description: The current status of the wire object.

WireAmount.description: The amount of the wire transaction in cents.

settlement_amount.description: Amount of money that moved across the Fedwire network from the instructing agent to the instructed agent.

settlement_currency_code.description: The currency code of the settlement amount.

instructed_amount.description: Amount of money to be moved between the debtor and creditor, before deduction of charges, expressed in the currency as ordered by the initiating party.

instructed_currency_code.description: The currency code of the instructed amount. A three-letter currency code as defined in ISO 4217.

instructed_to_settlement_exchange_rate.description: Factor used to convert an amount from the instructed currency to the settlement currency.

WirePartyAccountIdentifier.description: Object representing the account that the party holds at its financial institution

value.description: The value of the account identifier where the wire is being sent. For U.S. domestic wires, this should always be the full account number.

department.description: Identification of a division of a large organization or building.

sub_department.description: Identification of a sub-division of a large organization or building.

street_name.description: Name of a street or thoroughfare.

building_name.description: Name of the building or house.

floor.description: Floor or storey within a building.

post_box.description: Numbered box in a post office, assigned to a person or organisation, where letters are kept until called for.

post_code.description: Identifier consisting of a group of letters and/or numbers that is added to a postal address to assist the sorting of mail.

town_name.description: Name of a built-up area, with defined boundaries, and a local government.

town_location_name.description: Specific location name within the town. Note that this field is not commonly used for US-based addresses.

district_name.description: Identifies a subdivision within a country sub-division. Note that this field is not commonly used for US-based addresses.

country_sub_division.description: Identifies a subdivision of a country such as state, region, county.

line_one.description: Line one address of the party represented in an unstructured format.

WireOtherAgents.description: Details of any other agents involved in the wire transfer.

previous_instructing_agent_one.description: Object representing the “Previous Instructing Agent One”, which is a financial institution sitting in the payment chain between the Debtor Agent and the Instructing Agent.

previous_instructing_agent_two.description: Object representing the “Previous Instructing Agent Two”, which is a financial institution sitting in the payment chain between the Debtor Agent and the Instructing Agent.

previous_instructing_agent_three.description: Object representing the “Previous Instructing Agent Three”, which is a financial institution sitting in the payment chain between the Debtor Agent and the Instructing Agent.

intermediary_agent_two.description: Object representing the “Intermediary Agent Two”, which is a financial institution sitting in the payment chain between the Creditor Agent and the Instructed Agent.

intermediary_agent_three.description: Object representing the “Intermediary Agent Three”, which is a financial institution sitting in the payment chain between the Creditor Agent and the Instructed Agent.

message_identification.description: Point to point reference, as assigned by the instructing party, and sent to the next party in the chain to unambiguously identify the message..

end_to_end_identification.description: Unique identification that the initiating party in a wire message can use to unambiguously identify the transaction. This is passed through, unchanged, throughout the entire wire chain.

uetr.description: Universally unique identifier to provide an end-to-end reference of a payment transaction.

instruction_identification.description: Unique identification, as assigned by an instructing party for an instructed party, to unambiguously identify the instruction.

return_identification.description: Unique identification, as assigned by an instructing party for an instructed party, to unambiguously identify the returned transaction.

other_details.description: Free-formatted text for wire sender if `other` is selected for `type`.

charge_bearer.description: Specifies which party bears the charges for the wire transfer.

breakdown.description: Itemized list of charges assessed during wire transfer processing.

original_wire_id.description: If this wire is a return wire (i.e., returning a wire previously received), this field will provide the preceding wire's identifier (wire_id).

return_wire_ids.description: If this wire has been returned by any following wires, you can find the returning wire id(s) here.

WireReturnReasonEnum.description: Reason to return a wire payment.

reason.description: If wire is a return, this field will indicate the reason for the return

code.description: The return code for the wire return.

additional_information.description: If the return reason is NARR (stands for “narrative”), then an accompanying free-form explanation is required and will appear here. For other reasons, participants may optionally send additional information as well.

WireRejectionReasonEnum.description: Reason to return a wire payment.

IMAD.description: Input Message Accountability Data (IMAD), a unique identifier available to originating and receiving banks of a wire message.

reason.description: If a wire is rejected, this field will indicate the reason for the rejection.

additional_information.description: If the rejection reason is “narrative”, then an accompanying free-form explanation is required and will appear here. For other reasons, participants may optionally send additional information as well.

message_identification.description: If Lead rejected an incoming wire on your behalf, this field contains the message identification (formerly known as imad) of the return wire that we sent.

id.description: Unique identifier of the wire object.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 format timestamp representing when the wire object was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 format timestamp representing when the wire object was last updated.

settlement_date.description: The ISO-8601 Datestamp indicating the wire settlement date based on the Fedwire's business day definition.

type.description: Indicates the type of wire message that the object represents.

debtor.description: Details of the debtor sending funds.

debtor_agent.description: Details of the financial institution where the debtor (payer) holds their account. This object may be absent if the instructing agent does not provide this information.

instructing_agent.description: The financial institution that transmitted the payment through Fedwire. In some cases, this may differ from the bank where the account is held. This object will always be populated.

instructed_agent.description: The financial institution that received the payment through Fedwire. This may not always be the bank where the recipient's account is held. This object will always be populated.

creditor_agent.description: Details of the financial institution where the creditor (payee) holds their account. This object may be absent if the instructing agent does not provide this information.

creditor.description: Details of the creditor receiving funds.

ultimate_debtor.description: The ultimate party on whose behalf the payment is made, if different from the debtor.

ultimate_creditor.description: The ultimate party to which the payment is intended, if different from the creditor.

charges.description: Information about any charges assessed during wire transfer processing.

List of eligible return reason codes for wire transfers.
* `AC06` - account_blocked
* `AM09` - wrong_amount
* `BE01` - creditor_name_mismatch

IntendedRolesEnum.description:
The entity's intended role. Lead highly recommends using this field to confirm data requirements for different roles to minimize errors later. In the event more than 1 role is passed in and at least 1 role doesn’t pass validation, the request will not succeed. It will be an all or nothing behavior.
Lead currently supports:
* `authorized_user`: Individual entity with restricted access to the account, without legal responsibility. Only applies to individual entity.
* `authorized_signer`: Individual entity allowed to sign on behalf of the account holder, without legal responsibility. Only applies to individual entity.
* `account_holder`: Entity with full control and legal responsibility for the account. Applies to all entity types.

EntityRiskScoreEnum.description: The entity's risk score, mapped to Lead by the client. Client should have provided risk score mapping to Lead.

email.description: Must be a valid email address in the form `local-part@domain.tld`. The domain must contain at least one dot. Addresses using `localhost`, IP literals (e.g., user@[127.0.0.1]), or quoted local parts (e.g., "user"@example.com) are not accepted. Only common, real-world email formats are supported.

ContactMethodTypePhone.description: ContactMethodType = phone_number. Conforms to E.123 standard

phone_number.description: Primary phone number of the entity. Must follow E.123 format.

postal_code.description: Postal Code, required for US addresses. Please follow the following format: US: ^\d5(?:-\d4)?$, CN: ^\d6$, GB: ^[A-Z]{1,2}d[A-Z0-9]? ?d[A-Z]{2}$, JP: ^\d3-\d4$

state.description: State. required for US addresses in 2 character code (e.g. NY).

physical_address.description: Physical address for the entity. Physical addresses must not be P.O. boxes, registered agent addresses or in prohibited countries.

UsEntityTypeEnum.description: Entity is considered US entity if they have US tax identification. For US entities, US tax identification number as well as other available government-verified identification documents, if collected, are required.

TaxIdentificationTypeEnum.description:
The entity's US tax identification type.
- For `individual` entity, `ssn` or `itin` is required.
- For `business` entity, `ein` is required. For single-member LLC entities where no EIN is available, `ssn` or `itin` may be used.
- For `sole_prop` entity, `ssn` or `itin` or `ein` is required.

value.description: The entity's US tax identification number. Must be 9 digits (e.g. XXXXXXXXX). ssn must not start with a 9. ein must begin with a number between 00 and 99. itin must begin with a 9

IdentificationDocumentsTypeEnum.description:
The entity's government-verified identification document type. This will apply for identification types other than US tax identification.
- For `individual` entity, possible types are: `drivers_license`, `state_or_provincial_id`, `passport`, `permanent_residency_id`, `military_id`, `national_id`, `matriculate_id`, `other`. Note that `national_id` and `matriculate_id` are not applicable for `us_entity`.
- For `business` entity, possible types are: `tax_certificate`, `tax_returns`, `partnership_agreement`, `certificate_of_good_standing`, `formation_document`, `proof_of_place_on_stock_exchange`, `other`.
- For `sole_prop` entity, possible types are: `tax_certificate`, `tax_returns`, `name_certificate`, `drivers_license`, `state_or_provincial_id`, `passport`, `permanent_residency_id`, `military_id`, `national_id`, `matriculate_id`, `other`. Note that `national_id` and `matriculate_id` are not applicable for `us_entity`.

client_document_id.description: Client document ID for the identification document.

description.description: Description about the identification document. Required if identification document type is `other`.

identification_number.description: Unique identification number on the identification document.

expiration_date.description: Expiration date of the identification document.

issuing_state.description: Issuing state of the identification document. Must be 2 character code (e.g. NY). If the issuing country is US, this value must be a valid US State.

issuing_country.description: Issuing country of the identification document. ISO3166-1 Alpha-2 country code.

NonUsEntityTypeEnum.description: Entity is considered non-US entity if they do not have US tax identification. For non-US entities, at least 1 government-verified identification documents is required.

AdditionalDocumentTypeEnum.description: Type of additional non-government documents being provided to verify entity. Possible values are `bank_statement`, `utility_bills`, `proof_of_address`, `investigation_report`, `financial_statement`, `public_records`, `adverse_media`, `id_selfie`, `other`.

date_of_birth.description: Date of birth, in YYYY-MM-DD format. Authorized signers and account holders must be 18 years or older.

FundingCurrencyCode.description: A three-letter currency code as defined in ISO 4217. Must match the `currency_code` on the associated Subledger Balance object.

FundingStatus.description: The current status of the Funding object.

FundingRejectionReason.description: The rejection code that indicates the type of rejection. Note that Lead may introduce additional enums in the future; partner integrations should be ready to handle them as non-breaking changes.

FundingRejection.description: Object containing information relevant for cases where the Funding object is in a rejected status. When the object is not in a rejected status, fields will still be present with empty strings.

details.description: Additional info about the rejection. Reserved for future use.
details.example: Funding object rejected due to an internal error.

id.description: Unique ID of the funding object.

subledger_balance_id.description: Unique ID of the credit subledger balance object that a funding object is being created for. The referenced Subledger Balance must be in an `active` status. If the Subledger Balance has an `expected_maturity_date`, it must not be in the past.

principal.description:
The total amount of funding that is part of the loan product's principal. Note that upon funding, this amount will be considered disbursed, and Lead will begin applying imputed interest. All amounts will be assumed to be in the currency set on the Subledger Balance object.
Invariant: (external_disbursement_amount > 0) OR (withheld_amount > 0).

external_disbursement_amount.description: The portion of the principal amount that will be made available for external disbursement at the time of funding (in minor units). Either this field or `withheld_amount` must be greater than 0.

withheld_amount.description: The portion of the principal amount that will be withheld and not disbursed externally at the time of funding (in minor units). Either this field or `external_disbursement_amount` must be greater than 0.

merchant_origination_fee_amount.description: The amount of origination fees charged to the merchant that is included in the principal amount (in minor units). Must be less than `external_disbursement_amount` + `withheld_amount`.

other_fees.description: Fees relating to the funding request that Lead needs awareness of for bookkeeping purposes only.

borrower_origination_fee_amount.description: Non-capitalized origination fee charged to the borrower at the point of funding (in minor units).

metadata.description: Additional metadata associated with the funding.

created_at.description: The ISO-8601 timestamp at which the Funding object was created.

updated_at.description: The ISO-8601 timestamp at which the Funding object was last updated.

autopay_method.description: The autopay method. Required if `type` is `credit`.

is_interest_based.description: Indicates whether the Subledger Balance is interest-based. Required if `type` is `credit`.

effective_apr.description: The APR as disclosed to the borrower. Required if `type` is `credit`. Must have exactly 3 decimal places (e.g., `10.250`).

effective_interest_rate.description: The true annualized cost of borrowing, taking into account the interest rate and impact of compounding. Required if `type` is `credit`. Must have exactly 6 decimal places (e.g., `9.500000`).

interest_rate.description: For deposit products, the annualized interest rate offered to customers on this Subledger Balance. For credit products, the contractual annual rate of interest charged on the loan’s outstanding principal, without taking into account compounding frequency or additional fees. Also known as the stated interest rate or nominal interest rate. Required when `is_interest_based` is `true`. Must have exactly 6 decimal places (e.g., `9.990000`).

expected_payment_frequency.description:
The expected frequency with which customer payments will occur.

**Calendar-based frequencies:**
- `weekly` — Once per week.
- `biweekly` — Once every two weeks (26 payments/year).
- `semi_monthly` — Twice per month on fixed calendar days (24 payments/year).
- `monthly` — Once per calendar month.
- `quarterly` — Once every calendar quarter (4 payments/year).
- `semi_annually` — Once every 6 months (2 payments/year).
- `annually` — Once per calendar year.
- `business_daily` — Once per business day.

**Fixed-interval frequencies:**
- `fixed_interval` — Payments occur after a fixed number of elapsed days rather than on calendar-based dates. When using this value, `expected_payment_frequency_interval_days` is required.

**Non-time-based frequencies:**
- `maturity` — A single payment at loan maturity.
- `variable` — Repayment does not follow a predictable time-based cadence; instead, payments are event-driven (e.g., repayment occurs the next time funds enter the borrower's account).

expected_payment_frequency_interval_days.description:
The number of days between expected customer payments when the repayment schedule follows a fixed interval.

**Required** when `expected_payment_frequency` is `fixed_interval`. **Must not be present** when `expected_payment_frequency` is any other value.

expected_number_of_payments.description: The expected number of payments if the customer pays according to the loan schedule.

expected_first_payment_date.description: The expected date of the first customer repayment. If there is only one repayment please use this field.

expected_first_payment_amount.description: The expected amount of the first customer repayment expressed as an integer in minor units. If there is only one repayment please use this field.

expected_last_payment_date.description: The expected date of the last customer repayment.

expected_last_payment_amount.description: The expected amount of the last customer repayment expressed as an integer in minor units.

expected_payment_amount.description: The expected payment amount in each repayment expressed as an integer in minor units.

value.description: The number of units in the contractual term length.

unit.description: The unit of the contractual term length. E.g., for a term loan, this represents the contractual loan term from the date of origination to the expected maturity date.

expected_maturity_date.description: The expected maturity date of the loan. Must not be in the past when creating a Funding against this Subledger Balance.

max_funding_amount.description: The maximum funding amount as an integer in minor units. For a term loan, this would be equivalent to the total loan amount. Cumulative Funding requests against this Subledger Balance cannot exceed this amount.

expected_finance_charge_amount.description: The expected finance charge amount expressed as an integer in minor units.

For incoming return requests, you must respond by 11:30PM ET on the 10th business day. If you do not respond, we will respond on your behalf with a rejection at midnight.
For outgoing return requests, the network deadline for the counterparty is 12:00AM ET on the 10th business day.

name.description: Name of the counterparty financial institution.

routing_number.description: Routing number of the counterparty financial institution.

identifier_value_last_four.description: Last four digits of the identifier value of the originator. Please contact Lead if you need the full value.

identifier_value.description: Full value of the identifier value of the beneficiary.

MessageToBeneficiary.description: Message from the originator to the beneficiary. Lead will populate this field for outgoing returns based on the return reason.

return_identification.description: Reference to the payment return message.

previous_message_identifier.description: IMAD of the previous wire. Used to identify the original wire transaction of a return wire.

payment_notification.description: Indicates a preference for payment notification.

imad.description: IMAD of the returned wire this wire rejection is referring to.

amount.description: The rewards balance amount as an integer in minor units.

amount.description: The total amount that is currently under dispute as an integer in minor units.

fees.description: The past due fees amount as an integer in minor units.

effective_apr.description: The APR as disclosed to the borrower. Required if `type` is `credit`.

effective_interest_rate.description: The true annualized cost of borrowing, taking into account the interest rate and impact of compounding. Required if `type` is `credit`.

interest_rate.description: For deposit products, the annualized interest rate offered to customers on this Subledger Balance. For credit products, the contractual annual rate of interest charged on the loan’s outstanding principal, without taking into account compounding frequency or additional fees. Also known as the stated interest rate or nominal interest rate.

expected_maturity_date.description: The expected maturity date of the loan.

max_funding_amount.description: The maximum funding amount expressed as an integer in minor units. For a term loan, this would be equivalent to the total loan amount.

expected_finance_charge_amount.description: The expected finance charge amount expressed as an integer in minor units.

loan_amount.description: The underwritten loan amount that gets approved.

annual_percentage_rate.description: The APR disclosed to the borrower at the time of signing loan documents.

customer_date_of_birth.description: Customer’s date of birth, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

customer_is_mla.description: Is the user is subject to the Military Lending Act?

customer_ofac_result.description: Did the user pass OFAC at the time of loan validation.

customer_id.description: Cash App’s user or customer ID

id.description: Unique identifier of the wire object

type.description: The type of the wire object.

updated_at.description: The ISO-8601 timestamp at which the wire object was last updated.

uetr.description: Unique End-To-End Reference assigned by the sender.

was_returned.description: Indicates if the wire was returned.

account_number.description: The account number for the bank account.

updated_at.description: An ISO-8601 timestamp at which the Subledger Balance was last updated in Lead's system.
